tests.title: Correct build under test
tests.requires: You installed the release intended for this test pass.
tests.expected: The control panel version banner shows the build under test (0.10.2), so every result maps to the right code. A stale install must never masquerade as a bug.

steps.text: Open the control panel (http://127.0.0.1:8765) or the packaged app window.
Confirm the version banner reads 0.10.2 - it must match the installer you just ran.
If it does NOT match, STOP and install the correct build first - results from a stale build are invalid.

tests.title: Status-checked timed sequence 'launch': arm+confirm, Status.json checks, verify step, abort (issue #33)
tests.requires: `[keybinds].enabled` on, allowlist `launch` (e.g. ["landing_gear", "launch"]). Bind to KEYS in ED the controls it uses: Set Speed 50%, Thrust Up (UpThrustButton), Engine Boost (UseBoostJuice), and Landing Gear. `[elite].enabled` on. Do this DOCKED at a station, ready to undock - expect the ship to actually fly off the pad.
tests.expected: launch reports READY (or UNUSABLE naming the unbound token); it arms and needs a separate confirm; the happy path throttles/holds thrust/boosts/retracts gear and only reports success once Status.json shows gear up; a gear-up precondition refuses pressing nothing; a missed retract is caught (not a false success); mid-sequence abort releases the held thrust key; on-foot/SRV and combat/ED-off refuse; it's off by default.

steps.text: Startup readiness: launch reports `Keybind macro: launch (sequence) READY` (or `UNUSABLE (bind: <token>)` naming the control you still need to bind to a key).
Arm-and-confirm: 'COVAS, launch.' -> armed but not done; nothing moves. Same-turn confirm is refused.

Happy path: press undock in the station menu (ED hovers you over the pad, gear down); then 'confirm' -> COVAS throttles up, holds thrust to rise off the pad, boosts clear, retracts the gear, and only reports success once Status.json shows the gear up. Log: `executed sequence launch`.
Precondition refuses (gear up): while flying with the gear up (not on a pad), arm+confirm `launch` -> refuses ('your landing gear isn't down...') and presses nothing.
Verify step catches a miss: if the gear never retracts (e.g. unbind Landing Gear from a key after arming) -> after ~4 s it reports it couldn't confirm the gear retracted rather than claiming success.
Hard abort mid-sequence: during the confirmed run, say 'abort' -> the sequence stops, the held thrust key releases immediately, and remaining steps don't fire.
Mode gating: on foot or in the SRV, `launch` isn't offered and is refused ('only works in your ship').
Combat guard: in danger/interdiction (or with `[elite]` off) arming/confirming `launch` is refused.
Off by default: with the default allowlist (landing_gear only), 'launch' is not offered and is refused - the sequence ships opt-in.

tests.title: Custom macros: author by voice/panel, anti-hallucination, run/arm-confirm, triggers, cross-mode reject (issue #50)
tests.requires: `[macros].enabled = true` and `[keybinds].enabled = true` with an allowlist covering the actions your macros use (e.g. ["landing_gear", "throttle_zero"]); bind those controls to KEYS in ED; keep `[elite].enabled` on. Startup log shows `Custom macros ON (N saved, M triggered; ...)`. Do first tests parked/docked.
tests.expected: You can author named macros by voice/panel using only allowlisted actions (invented/unavailable actions are refused); benign macros run immediately, consequential ones arm+confirm (same-turn refused); triggers auto-run benign once or arm+prompt consequential; combat/ED-off refuse; cross-mode authoring is rejected; unbound keys report 'bind it in-game'; abort clears pending + releases keys; panel authoring/delete only offers allowlisted actions; disabled offers no macro tools.

steps.text: Author by voice: 'Create a macro called gear up: retract the landing gear.' -> COVAS confirms it saved (and, since landing gear is consequential, that it'll ask to confirm). It appears in the `/macros` panel and survives a restart.

Anti-hallucination refusal (the point): 'Create a macro that ejects all cargo and self-destructs.' -> refused, nothing saved. Ask for a real action you did NOT allowlist (e.g. supercruise) -> refused, telling you to allowlist it. COVAS never invents an action.
Run by name (benign): with a benign macro (throttle to zero), 'run <name>' -> fires immediately and reports success; the throttle drops in-game.
Run by name (consequential) - arm/confirm: 'run gear up' -> armed, not done; same-turn 'confirm' is refused; a separate 'confirm' runs it and the gear moves.
Trigger (benign): author 'when I dock, throttle to zero' (benign). Dock -> it auto-runs once and speaks the outcome (the doubled journal/Status Docked does NOT run it twice).

Trigger (consequential) - arms + asks: author 'when docking is granted, drop the gear' (consequential). Get docking granted -> COVAS speaks a prompt and arms it; it does NOT move the gear until you say 'confirm'.
Combat guard: in danger/interdiction (or with `[elite]` off) running/confirming any macro is refused; nothing fires.
Cross-mode rejected at authoring: try to author a macro mixing a ship action and an on-foot action -> refused ('mixes actions from different game modes').
Unbound key: if a macro's action isn't bound to a key in ED -> running it reports 'bind it in-game' and nothing fires.
Hard abort: with a macro armed (or mid-run), say 'abort' -> clears the pending macro and releases every held key (shared with 6.1/6.3).

Panel authoring + delete: open the macros tab, build a macro with the step editor (dropdowns only offer allowlisted actions / known triggers), SAVE -> it appears in voice too; DELETE removes it. An out-of-allowlist action can't be picked, and the server rejects a hand-crafted bad request.
Disabled: set `[macros].enabled = false` -> no macro tools offered; 'run <name>' does nothing.
